Researcher

Mixed Methods · Public Policy · Arts & Culture

who tells stories with data

I combine interviews, statistical analysis in R, and data visualization to produce findings that get used — with a focus on the financial, labor, and policy conditions that support long-term sustainability in the arts sector.

Selected Projects

01
Featured  AAAE 47th Annual Conference · May 2026

Toward Self-Reliance: Policy Intervention Points in Chicago's Nonprofit Theatre

Mixed-methods study examining revenue structures and financial sustainability across 457 U.S. nonprofit theater organizations. Interviews + national panel data.

Panel Regression Mixed Methods SMU DataArts Arts Policy
→
02

When More Funding Is Not Enough: Revenue Concentration & Labor Investment

Fixed-effects regression analysis on 4,131 observations showing how revenue concentration reduces personnel spending in nonprofit performing arts organizations.

Fixed Effects HHI Analysis Nonprofit Finance
→
03

Arts Education Access in Chicago: A Spatial & Predictive Analysis

GIS mapping, machine learning, and Shiny dashboard examining disparities in arts education access across Chicago neighborhoods.

GIS Machine Learning Shiny ACS Data
→
04

Trauma Care Access in Cook County: A Policy Memo

Policy analysis with BOTEC cost estimation examining gaps in trauma care access and recommending targeted interventions for Cook County.

Policy Memo BOTEC Analysis Public Health
→
